creator: jamie@bishopston.net
text:
Apologies for not replying sooner - I only stumbled on this now, it must have
been raised when my email was broken for 6 weeks, whilst I was away.

ANYWAY...

Unfortunately, there is no help I can offer - I can't even run linux binaries
at the moment - they all core dump since moving to fc4.

I didn't even submit this port in the first place(!) though I don't mind
still maintaining it, as much as I can.

The only things I can suggest, is

Try installing the port again - it now builds with the audio libraries too.

If you are still having problems, it could be due to the old version of our
linux-emulation - in which case, FreeBSD 7-current may be the way to go,
but I wouldn't recommend that.

I'd stick with flash7 for now - indeed, once and if flash9 is working properly
with both native and linux browsers, there will be no need to keep flash7 in
the ports.

Sorry I can't be of more help,

cheers,
Jamie
